definitions: Nocturnal wild cats native to the southwestern United States, Mexico, Central, and South America, characterized by a tawny coat with black spots and stripes.
Medium-sized, slender cat with a short tail and a distinctive pattern of spots and stripes.
examples: The ocelots stalked their prey under the cover of darkness.
Many ocelots are found in dense tropical forests.
The zoo has a new exhibit featuring ocelots.
